description_long: This is a two-story apartment building with a prominent front porch supported by white columns and a decorative railing. The facade features a combination of gray siding and brickwork at the base, with intricate shingle detailing on the upper front gable. The windows are symmetrically arranged, with large multi-pane windows on the front and smaller, evenly spaced windows along the sides. The roof is flat with two visible brick chimneys, and the building has a rectangular footprint with a covered porch extending from the front. The overall design suggests a blend of traditional and early 20th-century American residential architecture, with attention to ornamental details and functional outdoor space.
description: A two-story gray and white apartment building with a front porch, brick base, flat roof, and symmetrical windows. Decorative shingles and columns add traditional charm.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main entrance, covered porch with columns, decorative shingles, and large front windows.
description_view_1: Side view displaying the long gray siding, two chimneys, and evenly spaced windows along the length of the building.
description_view_2: Rear view highlighting the flat roof, back entrance, and two upper windows, with the porch visible on the lower level.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the same gray siding, brick base, and window arrangement as the other side, with porch details visible.
attribute_window_arrangement: Windows are symmetrically arranged on the front and sides, with large grouped windows on the front and evenly spaced single windows along the sides.
